name: Schwarzwaelder Kirschtorte (Black Forest Cher
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat eggs, sugar, and vanilla together until thick and fluffy, about 10 minutes.
2. Alternately fold melted chocolate and sifted flour into the egg mixture, ending with flour. Pour the batter into three greased and floured 8-inch cake pans.
3. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 10 to 15 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool cakes in pans for 5 minutes; turn out on racks to cool completely.
4. Make syrup by mixing together sugar and water, boiling for 5 minutes. When syrup has cooled, stir in kirsch. Prick the cake layers and pour syrup over all three layers.
5. To make the butter-cream filling, beat together confectioners' sugar and unsalted butter until well blended. Add egg yolk; beat until light and fluffy, about 3 to 5 minutes. Fold in kirsch.
6. Assemble the cake by placing one layer on a cake plate. Spread with butter-cream filling. Using about 3/4 cup of the cherries (patted dry), drop cherries evenly over cream. Place second layer on top. Repeat with third layer. Fold 2 tablespoons confectioners' sugar into whipped cream and cover sides and top of the cake with it.
7. Decorate the top of the cake with remaining cherries.
8. To make chocolate curls from the chocolate bar, shave at room temperature with a vegetable peeler. Refrigerate curls until ready to use. Press chocolate curls on sides of the cake and sprinkle a few on the top. Chill.
